Working from: —
🗓 Game Start Date ▸
Set the date when the game officially begins. Until that date:
  • Daily and weekly automatic updates will be skipped
  • The Actions tab will show a countdown timer and be locked for all players
Leave blank to allow updates and actions immediately.
STEP 0: Setup ▸
Test as Player (Impersonation)
Temporarily act as any player to test their view — fog of war, actions, dashboard, and map all reflect their perspective. You stay logged in as admin; no data is changed.
Assign user to player
Create player
Create a new player record in the working dataset for Step 0 setup. It will be available immediately in assignments and trade controls.
Starting province assignment
Assign the selected provinces as starting territory for that player. This updates ownership, trade nodes, income, and exports automatically.
Fix exports
Scans every player's owned provinces and adds any missing exports derived from original_export. Safe to run multiple times — won't duplicate existing entries.
Border Detection (Ray-Cast)
For each trade-node province, shoots rays at 0°→359° (1° steps) from its SVG centre. The first province each ray touches becomes a border neighbour. Updates live border data and lets you copy the JSON to make it permanent. Open the Map tab first so the SVG is loaded.
Add Sea Crossing
Creates a bidirectional sea crossing between two provinces. This will be saved to Firebase and displayed as arrows on the map.
🕵️ Espionage Monitor ▸
Tab Access — Players who can see Espionage
Based on ≥50% ownership of province #90 Malacca, #153 Khorasan, #42 Panama, #165 the Levant, or #99 Canton.
Load game data to see access list.
Espionage Centers (Province-Based)
Centers = access provinces owned (≥50%). Each reduces Perform Espionage cost by 1 (base cost 6, min 1). No admin grant needed — province ownership determines centers automatically.
Load game data to see centers.
STEP 1: Trades & Adjustments ▸
Export & Eco Point Trading
Spend Eco Points
War Start
War End
Alliance Create
Alliance Remove
Add Player to Alliance
Remove Player from Alliance
Add Player to War
Remove Player from War
Alliance War Sync
Checks every war — if a player is in it, their alliance members are added too.
Remove Player
This will completely remove the player from all data: nations list, province ownership, alliances, wars, and exports.
Auto-Prune Dead Nations
Automatically deletes all nations with 0 owned provinces (≥50%), then removes any wars where one side has no remaining participants.
STEP 2: Battles ▸
Setup — or enter manually
Attackers
Defenders
Positive = attackers win, negative = defenders win, 0 = draw
Military & Defence Changes
Province Redistribution
Export Redistribution
STEP 3: Daily Update ▸
What this does:
  1. Decreases happiness by 3% per active war (max 15% per day regardless of war count)
  2. Increments war day counters by 1 for all active wars
  3. Decrements truce day counters by 1; removes expired truces
  4. Applies subsidy bonuses (+5% × eco pts) and increments subsidy day counters
STEP 4: Weekly Update ▸
What this does (in order):
  1. Food −1% per military pt; stability −1% per economic pt (happiness handled by daily update)
  2. Applies export bonuses to food, stability, and happiness
  3. Export-driven pop stats consume eco income at a 1:0.25 conversion rate (e.g. 4% food from exports costs 1 eco income)
  4. Adds province income (econ & mil) for provinces ≥50% owned
  5. Resets defence to the province def-income total
  6. Applies trade node penalty if applicable
  7. If econ > 50, decays excess by 10%; if mil > 50, decays mil by 10%
STEP 5: Review & Upload ▸

Export the admin workbook or upload the data from your Excel file and admin changes to Firebase.

🏆 Hall of Nations

Loading rankings…
📜 Game Rules
Everything you need to know to play.
⚔️ Fighting & Wars (Combat)
🎲
How combat works: Military points are your attack value. When you launch an attack, the game rolls a random whole number between 1 and your total military points. The defender does the same, but also adds their province's Defence points directly to their roll. If your attack roll matches or beats theirs, you win and grab provinces!
💥
Military losses: Win or lose, every country that fought in a battle instantly loses 10% of their military points due to frontline chaos.
🏚️
Province damage: The specific province that got hit takes structural damage — its standing defence baseline is chopped by 25% right after the battle.
😞
War morale drain: Being at war kills public morale. For every active war you are in, you lose 3% Happiness every day. If you are a total warmonger, this caps out at a maximum loss of 15% Happiness a day, no matter how many separate countries you are fighting.
🤝 Alliances
🗺️
You can form an alliance with up to 5 people across the map.
⚔️
When you attack, you fight as one — military stacks across all alliance members.
🛡️
When someone attacks you, only one person's defence applies — choose your defensive position wisely.
📉 The Overhoarding Tax (High-Wealth Slashes)
💰
Eco cap: If your Economic points go above 50, you get slapped with a wealth tax and lose 10% of your total bank balance at the end of the week.
💀
Military cap: If your Military points go over 50, 10% of your troops automatically decay at the end of the week.
🌾 Population Welfare & Weekly Drains
🍖
Troops gotta eat: Food decreases every week based on how big your army is — 1 Military point drains 1% Food per week.
📊
Economic strain: Stability decreases every week based on your wealth — 1 Economic point drains 1% Stability per week.
🔄
Defence resets weekly: Defence points do not stack or carry over. At the end of each week, your defence completely resets back to the baseline given by your currently owned provinces.
🚨 The 20% Crisis Danger Zone
If any welfare stat drops below 20%, your country enters full meltdown mode and production freezes:
🍽️
Below 20% Food — Famine
Your people are starving. Your weekly Military income is set to 0.
🔥
Below 20% Stability — Riots
The government loses control. Your weekly Economic income is set to 0.
✊
Below 20% Happiness — Strikes
Your workforce is unionizing. Both Economic and Military income are cut in half (50% penalty).
🏦 The Marketplace & Trading
💱
Base conversion rate: Buying welfare stats (Food, Stability, Happiness) with your Eco points starts at a base rate of 1 : 1.
📈
Supply & demand: The market reacts to server-wide buying. There is a weekly demand scale of 20 units and a sensitivity factor of 0.6. The more units of a resource the entire server buys in a single week, the worse the conversion rate gets — making it way more expensive for the next buyer.
🚫
Rate floor: The rate has a hard floor of 0.05 so it never hits absolute zero — but if everyone spam-buys the same resource, the market will effectively dry up!
🕵️ Espionage
🔍
What is espionage? Espionage allows players to perform various covert actions related to gathering intelligence — things normal diplomacy can't give you. It's about knowing more than your enemies do.
🏛️
Unlocking espionage: Espionage access is unlocked once you control at least 5 espionage centre provinces. Below that threshold the entire espionage tab stays locked — build your network first.
🎭
Impersonation: Any espionage-capable player can impersonate another player for 30 seconds at a cost of 10 ECO. During that window you see their exact map view, access their chat channels, and can send messages that appear under their name. It's a powerful intelligence tool — use it wisely.
💸
Cost scaling: Most espionage actions cost 6 minus the number of espionage centres you own in ECO — so the more centres you hold, the cheaper your ops become. Expand your network and your spies get better funded.
🌫️ Fog of War
👁️
Limited vision: The fog of war means you can only gather real-time data from provinces close to your own territory. Provinces far from your borders show stale, outdated intel — you won't know what's really going on over there.
🔭
Lifting the fog: Under certain conditions — primarily by spending ECO through espionage — you can temporarily lift the fog entirely and gain full map visibility for 24 hours. This is expensive but can be game-changing at the right moment.
😤
Yes, it's intentional: The fog of war can feel limiting and at times deeply annoying — that's entirely by design. Information asymmetry is a core part of the game. Not knowing what your neighbours are up to creates tension, drives diplomacy, and makes espionage genuinely valuable. IT'S A FEATURE. TRUST.
💀 Elimination & Rankings
🗺️
You die when you lose all provinces. The moment a battle leaves you with zero provinces (≥50% ownership), your nation is eliminated. There is no coming back.
📊
Hall of Nations: Every eliminated player is added to the 🏆 Rankings tab, showing how long they survived (days), their peak province count, and a summary of who finished them off.
🏆
Win condition: The last nation standing wins the game. When only one player has provinces left, they are declared the winner and all remaining players see a victory screen.
🔒 Log in to use Chat.
⛔ You must be assigned a player nation to use Chat.
